revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:陶棺]] [[File:陶棺.JPG|thumb|300px|right|The three caskets in the foreground are Haji-type tortoiseshell-shaped, and the one in the back is Sue-type four-sided roof-shaped casket (at the Tsuyama Local Museum in {{ill|Tsuyama|en|Tsuyama|qid=Q841125|lt=Tsuyama|1=Tsuyama}}).]] '''Tōkan''' (陶棺) refers to a type of {{ill|Coffin|en|Coffin|qid=Q184418|lt=coffin|1=Coffin}} made by firing {{ill|Clay|en|Clay|qid=Q42302|lt=clay|1=Clay}}. This article focuses on the tōkan used in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == Overview == Tōkan appear in late to terminal {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofuns|1=Kofun}}, and they can be made of either {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} or {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. The shapes are classified into tortoiseshell-shaped and house-shaped forms. Among the house-shaped tōkan, the roof-shaped lid is either {{ill|kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kirizuma-style|qq=}} or {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|qid=Q162034|lt=yotsumune-style|1=Hip roof}}. The bottom part often features multiple hollow cylindrical legs. The emergence of tōkan was facilitated by the development of {{ill|ceramic technology|ja|窯業|1=Kilnwork|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic technology|13=User:Immanuelle/Kilnwork|qq=}} and they are widely distributed in the {{ill|Kinki region|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q164256}} and {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|qid=Q127864|lt=Chūgoku region|1=Chūgoku region}}. == Kofun where tōkan have been excavated == === Kinki region === * {{ill|Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Shinoda Senzuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Shinoda Chitsuka Kofun Cluster|ja|信太千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Chitsuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kofun cluster|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609514}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|qid=Q696412|lt=Izumi|1=Izumi, Osaka}}) * {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara|1=Kashiwara}}, Osaka Prefecture) * {{ill|Doyama Kofun|ja|堂山古墳群 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Daitō, Osaka|en|Daitō, Osaka|qid=Q386668|lt=Daitō|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture) * {{ill|Satsukigaoka Kofun|ja|五月丘古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsukigaoka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五月ヶ丘古墳}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture) === Chūgoku region === * {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|qid=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun}} ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|qid=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}) == Gallery == File:井之上古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan excavated from {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{small|Fuefuki, Yamanashi Prefecture. Displayed at Kokugakuin University Museum.|qq=}} File:歌姫横穴出土 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan excavated from Uta Hime 1st Burial Cave
{{small|Nara City, Nara Prefecture. Displayed at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum.|qq=}} File:赤田横穴群5号墓 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan excavated from Akada Burial Cave 5
{{small|Nara City, Nara Prefecture. Photographed during an exhibition at Osaka Prefecture Chikatsu-Asuka Museum.|qq=}} File:美作市野形出土 陶棺 (J-2673).JPG|Tōkan excavated from Nogata, Mimasaka
{{small|Mimasaka, Okayama Prefecture. Displayed at the Tokyo National Museum.|qq=}} File:京都市西京区石見上里出土 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan excavated from Nishikyo-ku, Ishimi, Kyoto
{{small|Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. Displayed at Kyoto University Museum.|qq=}} File:北平尾1号墳出土 須恵質四注式陶棺.JPG|Sue-type tōkan excavated from Kitahirao 1st Kofun
{{small|Nagaokakyo, Kyoto Prefecture. Displayed at Nagaokakyo City Cultural Heritage Center.|qq=}} File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan excavated from {{ill|Satsukigaoka Kofun|ja|五月丘古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsukigaoka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五月ヶ丘古墳}}
{{small|Ikeda City, Osaka Prefecture. Displayed at Ikeda City Folk Museum.|qq=}} File:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 漆塗陶棺.JPG|Lacquered tōkan excavated from {{ill|Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Ikaruga, Nara Prefecture. Displayed at the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum.|qq=}} File:岡山県美作市平福出土 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan excavated from Hirafuku, Mimasaka
{{small|Mimasaka, Okayama Prefecture. Displayed at the Tokyo National Museum.|qq=}} File:本坊山古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|Tōkan excavated from {{ill|Honbōyama Kofun|ja|本坊山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honbōyama Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Setouchi, Okayama Prefecture. Displayed at the Tokyo National Museum.|qq=}} File:赤田横穴群9号墓 円筒形陶棺.JPG|Cylindrical tōkan excavated from Akada Burial Cave 8
{{small|Nara City, Nara Prefecture. Photographed during an exhibition at Osaka Prefecture Chikatsu-Asuka Museum.|qq=}} File:群馬県太田市太田字亀山出土 小型陶棺 (J-6024).JPG|Small tōkan excavated from Ota, Gunma Prefecture
{{small|Displayed at the Tokyo National Museum.|qq=}} File:岡山県津山市河辺出土 小型陶棺 (J-37293).JPG|Small tōkan excavated from Kawabe, Tsuyama
{{small|Tsuyama, Okayama Prefecture. Displayed at the Tokyo National Museum.|qq=}} == References == * Tamura Kōichi and Gōda Yoshimasa (Eds.), ''Kōkogaku Tanshō no Kiso Yōgo'' (Basic Terms for Archaeological Exploration), Yamakawa Shuppansha, July 2000, pp. 95–96. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Jar burial|en|Jar burial|qid=Q4244233|lt=Jar burial|1=Jar burial}} * {{ill|Sabukaze Kiln Sites|en|Sabukaze Kiln Sites|qid=Q11457448|lt=Kanpū Ancient Kiln Site Group|1=Sabukaze Kiln Sites}} * {{ill|Sarcophagus of the Spouses|en|Sarcophagus of the Spouses|qid=Q2294393|lt=Sarcophagus of the Spo|1=Sarcophagus of the Spouses}} {{ill|Sarcophagus of the Spouses|en|Sarcophagus of the Spouses|qid=Q2294393|lt=u|1=Sarcophagus of the Spouses}} {{ill|Sarcophagus of the Spouses|en|Sarcophagus of the Spouses|qid=Q2294393|lt=ses|1=Sarcophagus of the Spouses}} - Refer to {{ill|National Etruscan Museum of Villa Giulia#Collections|ja|ヴィラ・ジュリア国立博物館#収蔵品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Etruscan Museum of Villa Giulia#Collections|1=National Etruscan Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15055388}}. {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|陶棺|version=100462198|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:陶棺]] {{Commons category|Pottery coffins from Japan}} {{wikidata link|Q18337447}} [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Coffins]] [[Category:遺構]]
